title: Pilot contribution end-to-end, merged upstream
context: The M0 pilot is the first end-to-end contribution that proves the full pipeline works: gate check, issue-first alignment, authoring against templates, reproducible environment pinning, License+Data review, Technical review, and upstream merge with an outcome artifact recorded. The pilot is deliberately chosen to maximize merge probability: an OSI-permissive (MIT/BSD/Apache) tool, actively maintained, DCO-only (no broad CLA), maintainer-labelled gap, and needing only shipped or synthetic data so it stays riskTier low and requires no Domain reviewer. Depends on all M0 foundation tasks: reviewer-001 (blocking reviewer filled), gate-002, template-003, repro-004, catalog-005, and outreach-006.
objective: Execute one complete contribution end-to-end — from gate check through to upstream merge — for a Priority A tool, following all templates and the canonical contribution model, with the outcome artifact recorded at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-pilot-007.json.
acceptanceCriteria: Target tool selected with a realistic merge path: OSI-permissive (MIT/BSD/Apache), actively maintained, DCO (not broad-CLA), with a maintainer-labelled 'good first issue' or 'docs/tests wanted' gap, needing only shipped or synthetic data.
Gate-002 (both axes) passed with the artifact committed before any authoring begins.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer before authoring, referencing the outreach-006 shortlist.
The docs/tests/tutorial follows the templates (template-003); any tutorial runs reproducibly in CI from the pinned environment (reproEnv.ciRunRef recorded); provenance/citations present.
The contribution is merged upstream — or, if not merged, submitted with the specific blocker surfaced and documented.
Outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-pilot-007.json recording merge-commit URL (or submission URL + blocker) and coverage before/after.
output: A contribution merged upstream (or submitted with blocker surfaced) for a Priority A open-source cancer research tool, with the gate artifact, provenance, and outcome record committed — proving the full M0 pipeline works end-to-end.
